questions.q: In which field of science did Jayant Narlikar primarily work?
questions.explanation: Jayant Narlikar was an Indian astrophysicist best known for his work in cosmology and theories of gravity.

questions.q: Narlikar is famous for being a critic of which widely accepted cosmological model?
questions.explanation: Narlikar was a prominent advocate of alternatives to the Big Bang, supporting steady-state and quasi-steady-state cosmologies.

questions.q: Narlikar co-developed a theory of gravity with which British astronomer?
questions.explanation: The Hoyle-Narlikar theory of gravity was developed jointly with the British astronomer Fred Hoyle.

questions.q: Which research institute in Pune did Narlikar found and serve as its first director?
options: IUCAA (Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics)
The Tata Institute of Fundamental Research
questions.explanation: Narlikar founded the Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics (IUCAA) in Pune and was its founding director.

questions.q: In which country was Jayant Narlikar born?
questions.explanation: Narlikar was born in Kolhapur, India, in 1938.

questions.q: At which famous university did Narlikar study and work with Fred Hoyle as a young researcher?
questions.explanation: Narlikar studied at the University of Cambridge, where he collaborated closely with Fred Hoyle.

questions.q: Besides research, Narlikar was widely known in India for which public activity?
questions.explanation: Narlikar was a celebrated science populariser, writing books, articles, and science fiction for general audiences.

questions.q: Which high civilian honour of India was conferred on Narlikar?
questions.explanation: Narlikar received the Padma Bhushan and later the Padma Vibhushan for his contributions to science.

questions.q: The Hoyle-Narlikar theory is an alternative formulation of which fundamental force?
questions.explanation: The Hoyle-Narlikar theory is an alternative theory of gravitation, drawing on Mach's principle.

questions.q: Narlikar wrote popular fiction in which genre, often in Marathi and English?
questions.explanation: Narlikar wrote acclaimed science fiction, helping bring scientific ideas to a broad readership.

questions.q: In which year was Jayant Narlikar born?
questions.explanation: Jayant Vishnu Narlikar was born on 19 July 1938 in Kolhapur.

questions.q: Narlikar's father, Vishnu Vasudeva Narlikar, was a distinguished figure in which field?
questions.explanation: His father was a noted mathematician and a professor who worked on the general theory of relativity.

questions.q: The Hoyle-Narlikar theory was strongly influenced by which principle concerning the origin of inertia?
questions.explanation: The theory incorporated Mach's principle, the idea that local inertia arises from the influence of all the matter in the universe.

questions.q: Before founding IUCAA, Narlikar headed the Theoretical Astrophysics Group at which Mumbai institution?
options: The Indian Institute of Technology Bombay
The Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR)
questions.explanation: On returning to India, Narlikar led the Theoretical Astrophysics Group at TIFR in Mumbai before establishing IUCAA in Pune.

questions.q: IUCAA, the centre Narlikar founded, is located on the campus of which university?
questions.explanation: IUCAA is situated on the campus of Savitribai Phule Pune University (formerly the University of Pune).

questions.q: Narlikar later extended steady-state ideas into which model proposed with Hoyle and Geoffrey Burbidge?
questions.explanation: Along with Fred Hoyle and Geoffrey Burbidge, Narlikar developed the quasi-steady-state cosmology as an alternative to the Big Bang.

questions.q: What does the abbreviation IUCAA stand for?
options: Indian Union Centre for Aeronautics and Astronautics
Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics
International University Council for Applied Astronomy
Indian University Consortium for Atomic Astrophysics
questions.explanation: IUCAA stands for the Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics, set up to support university astronomy in India.

questions.q: Narlikar received an early international honour, the Adams Prize, while at which institution?
questions.explanation: Narlikar shared the prestigious Adams Prize at Cambridge in 1967 for his work in mathematics and cosmology.

questions.q: Which Indian academy honour for science writing did Narlikar receive in 2014?
questions.explanation: Narlikar received the Sahitya Akademi Award in 2014 for his Marathi autobiography, recognising his literary contributions.

questions.q: In broad terms, the steady-state cosmology Narlikar championed holds that the universe, on large scales, is:
options: Roughly unchanging in appearance over time
Only a few thousand years old
questions.explanation: Steady-state cosmology proposes that the universe looks essentially the same at all times, with continuous creation of matter compensating for expansion.

questions.q: Narlikar earned international fame at a young age partly through a 1964 Royal Society presentation of a new gravity theory developed with which mentor?
questions.explanation: The 1964 presentation of the Hoyle-Narlikar theory of gravity at the Royal Society in London, widely reported in the press, brought the young Narlikar international recognition.

questions.q: What was Jayant Narlikar's full name including his middle name?
questions.explanation: His full name was Jayant Vishnu Narlikar, his middle name coming from his father Vishnu Vasudeva Narlikar.

questions.q: The Hoyle-Narlikar theory reformulated gravity using which mathematical approach to interactions?
questions.explanation: The theory used an action-at-a-distance, direct inter-particle formulation inspired by the Wheeler-Feynman absorber theory of electrodynamics.

questions.q: In quasi-steady-state cosmology, the continuous creation of matter is described by what kind of hypothetical field?
questions.explanation: Hoyle, Narlikar and Burbidge invoked a 'creation field' or C-field to account for the continuous creation of matter in the universe.

questions.q: The Wheeler-Feynman absorber theory that inspired Hoyle-Narlikar gravity originally concerned which interaction?
questions.explanation: The Wheeler-Feynman absorber theory was a time-symmetric, action-at-a-distance formulation of electromagnetism, which Hoyle and Narlikar adapted to gravity.

questions.q: In which year did Narlikar establish IUCAA in Pune?
questions.explanation: IUCAA was established in 1988 under the University Grants Commission, with Narlikar as its founding director.

questions.q: Narlikar was associated with a controversial 2001 experiment that sampled which environment for microorganisms?
questions.explanation: Narlikar led balloon experiments sampling the stratosphere for microorganisms, motivated by panspermia ideas associated with Hoyle and Wickramasinghe.

questions.q: Narlikar completed his undergraduate studies before Cambridge at which Indian university?
questions.explanation: Narlikar took his BSc at Banaras Hindu University, where his father was a professor, before heading to Cambridge.

questions.q: The conformal invariance built into the Hoyle-Narlikar framework gives masses of particles what kind of origin?
options: A dependence on all other matter in the universe
An origin fixed only by the strong force
questions.explanation: In Hoyle-Narlikar theory the mass of a particle is generated by its interaction with all other matter, realizing Mach's principle through a conformally invariant formulation.

questions.q: Narlikar served as president of which body promoting public understanding of cosmology and astronomy?
options: The cosmology commission of the International Astronomical Union
The Indian National Science Academy presidency for life
questions.explanation: Narlikar chaired the International Astronomical Union's commission on cosmology, reflecting his global standing in the field.
